description: Add `gbiv tmux refresh` command that syncs an existing tmux session's windows to the active color set from GBIV.md and reorders them into canonical ROYGBIV order.

userStories.title: Create refresh.rs with clap subcommand definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to scaffold the `refresh` subcommand entry point so that clap can route `gbiv tmux refresh` to the new handler.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/commands/tmux/refresh.rs` exists and exports `refresh_subcommand()` returning a `clap::Command` named `refresh`
`refresh_subcommand()` is registered in the tmux subcommand group in `src/commands/tmux/mod.rs`
Running `gbiv tmux refresh --help` prints usage without panicking
Running `gbiv tmux` with no subcommand prints help listing `refresh` as an available subcommand
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement precondition guards in refresh_command()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `refresh_command()` to validate that tmux is installed, that the CWD is inside a gbiv project, and that the target session exists, so that the command fails fast with actionable errors.
acceptanceCriteria: When tmux is not on PATH, the command returns an error containing 'tmux not found. Please install tmux.'
When run outside a gbiv project, the command returns an error explaining no gbiv project was found
When the session named after the folder does not exist, the command returns an error suggesting `gbiv tmux new-session`
All error paths exit with non-zero status
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Derive active color set from GBIV.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse GBIV.md and collect the distinct set of recognized ROYGBIV color tags, always including `main`, so that subsequent steps know which windows to manage.
acceptanceCriteria: Colors present as tags on feature entries in GBIV.md are included in the active set
`main` is always in the active set regardless of GBIV.md content
Tags that are not in the ROYGBIV list (e.g., `purple`) are excluded from the active set
When GBIV.md is missing or has no tagged features, the active set contains only `main`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create missing windows for active colors
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the refresh command to create a tmux window for each active color not already present in the session, so that the session reflects current GBIV.md state.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing session windows are queried by name via `tmux list-windows -F '#{window_name}'`
A window is created with `tmux new-window` for each active color not already in the session
Each new window's working directory is set to `<gbiv-root>/<color>/<folder-name>/`
If a worktree path does not exist on disk, a warning is printed to stderr and that window is skipped
Windows that already exist in the session are not re-created
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Reorder session windows into canonical ROYGBIV order
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the refresh command to move managed windows into canonical order (`main` first, then active colors in ROYGBIV sequence) with unmanaged windows appended at the end, so that the session layout is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: After refresh, managed windows appear in order: `main`, then active colors in ROYGBIV sequence
Windows not in the managed set (user-created) appear after all managed windows, preserving their relative order
When windows are already in canonical order, the command completes without error
Reordering uses `tmux move-window` (does not destroy existing windows or their shell state)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Print success summary and wire up all tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the refresh command to print a success summary on completion and have full unit test coverage, so that users get clear feedback and regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: On success, the command prints a message indicating how many windows were created and exits with status 0
Unit test: `refresh_command` errors when tmux is not on PATH
Unit test: `refresh_command` errors when not inside a gbiv project
Unit test: active color derivation — unrecognized tags excluded, `main` always present
Unit test: window creation skipped for colors already in the session
Unit test: warning emitted and window skipped when worktree path missing
Unit test: window order after reorder matches canonical ROYGBIV sequence with unmanaged windows at end
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
